nodes.text: You there. I'm Commander Lirien Vale, and I've been waiting for someone with actual field potential to walk through that gate. The Aethel Gardens used to be the crown jewel of Xylos — terraced groves fed by the Cradle's overflow, bioluminescent at night, one of the few things that survived the Severance mostly intact. Now they're choking with Forest Slimes, and every convoy we lose to the ooze is another crack in Concordium's supply chain.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The Slimes aren't random, Operative. Before the Severance, this soil was saturated with clean aethel energy from the Cradle's natural overflow. When the Siphon overloaded and the Shard shattered, that overflow became contaminated — every root, every organism that absorbed it twisted into something new. The Slimes are what the Gardens' old microbiome became after three hundred years of Severance corruption. The Great Schism cost me half my garrison to Iron Hegemony defectors, so I'm running this outpost lean. These supply routes are the only uncontested corridor into Nexus Core. They fall, the whole Concordium operation gets squeezed.
Every route you clear is a vein we restore to the Cradle. Defeat five Forest Slimes and bring me a gel sample — the research division wants the contamination profile. For Nexus. Move out, Operative.

The corridor is clearer, but the outpost perimeter is now surrounded on three sides by Aethel Sproutlings — corrupted seedlings that haven't seen clean sunlight since before the Schism. They've been pressing in from the northern grove since dawn, and two of my guards reported cuts through standard-issue armor plating. The perimeter must hold.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: This outpost was built to serve as the Gardens' forward monitoring station — we track aethel output fluctuations and relay them to Nexus Core for calibration. If these Sproutlings breach the perimeter and reach the monitoring equipment, we lose visibility on the entire eastern corridor. After the Schism, when the Iron Hegemony faction broke away and took a third of our engineering staff, we lost the ability to run redundant systems. There is no backup monitoring station. This one is it.
Four Sproutlings on the perimeter line, one minor essence sample for biological documentation. The Concordium does not cede ground it has already paid for. Hold the line.

We have a structural problem. The bio-conductive wiring running through the Gardens' eastern grove — fifteen years of installation work, the same wiring that channels aethel energy from the Gardens into Nexus Core — is being eaten alive. Garden Mites have bored into the conduit walls and they're feeding on the wiring's energy conduction layer. If the eastern grove fails, we lose primary power routing to three Nexus sectors.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The bio-conductive wiring was an innovation of the Concordium's founding engineers — the same generation who first successfully reactivated the Siphon after the Severance. It took fifteen years because the original Aethelgardian conduit infrastructure had been shattered along with everything else. These Mites evolved specifically to consume that wiring — which means they've been a problem since the conduits were first laid. We've been managing the infestation for years. This incursion is worse. I cannot write the report that explains we lost Nexus Core power because of insects.

Six Garden Mites in the eastern grove, three chitin samples for the engineering division — they want to analyze what the Mites are extracting from the wiring. Everything is data, Operative. Everything.

The aethel conduits in the eastern corridor are humming at the wrong frequency — lower, dragging, like something has changed their internal structure. Our engineering team found the cause: Feral Rootlings have grown through the conduit walls and they're drinking raw aethel energy directly from the lines. They weren't in any pre-Severance bestiary. They're something the conduits themselves created.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The conduits were carved by the Concordium's founders — people who rebuilt the Aethel Cradle from ruins and wept when it first activated. They ran the conduit lines through the Gardens' root systems to take advantage of the organic conductivity. The Rootlings were probably ordinary plant life before they contacted raw aethel. Three hundred years of feeding on the conduit's energy has warped them into something that exists only in this place, dependent on the infrastructure they're destroying. If the conduit lines fail tonight, Nexus Core goes dark. The Arcanum will be on the doorstep before morning.

Six Feral Rootlings from the conduit sections — cut them out. Four living root samples for the engineering division's biological database. The Concordium does not discard knowledge. Not even from the things trying to kill us.

Commander Vale's field reports reached Nexus Core faster than you'd expect. I'm Instructor Kael — the Concordium's master trainer, a veteran of the earliest post-Schism skirmishes when the Hegemony's defection was still raw and every patrol felt like it could become a civil war. Vale says you have field potential worth shaping. The Concordium rebuilt the Aethel Cradle from ruins. It did not do that with generalists. Before you go further, you need to choose your path.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The Concordium recognizes three combat philosophies forged in the crucible of post-Schism survival. The Shield holds the line and absorbs damage so others don't have to — the Schism taught us that fortified ground is the difference between survival and collapse. The Blade moves fast and strikes before the enemy can adapt — because the Hegemony's defection proved that waiting too long costs everything. The Conduit channels aethel energy as force, reading the battlefield and amplifying the team — because the Cradle's power is not just for industry. Every Concordium operative makes this choice. The wrong answer will not be punished. But it will define every engagement you fight from this day forward.

Speak with me at the training station, review your class selection, then put your calibration to the test against three Forest Slimes in the field. The Concordium's future is built on individuals who chose wisely. Choose well, Operative.

Welcome to the Broken Civil Quarter — what's left of it. Before the Great Schism, this was the beating heart of unified Nexus, where the Concordium's administrative and engineering divisions worked side by side in buildings that actually had intact roofs. The Schism tore this district apart as badly as it tore the faction apart. Concordium engineers have located a viable breach point into the main reclamation corridor. But Tunnel Crawlers have nested in it.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The Civil Quarter's architecture is layered history. The upper buildings were built post-Severance by the original unified Concordium — the same faction that rebuilt the Cradle. The lower infrastructure, the tunnel system, dates back to before the Severance, possibly Aethelgardian construction. Tunnel Crawlers evolved in those lower tunnels, isolated from whatever happened above for centuries. They're not aggressive by nature — they're territorial, which means the breach point we've identified is directly through their primary nesting territory. There is no alternative route. We stake the Concordium's flag in this district, or we cede it to whatever faction moves faster.

Six Tunnel Crawlers from the main breach, one chitin sample for materials documentation. Every street we reclaim in this district is a reminder that unity was once possible here. Clear it. For the Cradle.

We have a retrieval problem. The original unified Concordium buried supply caches throughout the Civil Quarter — medical supplies, engineering manuals, communication arrays — against the day the Schism might be reversed and the faction reunified. Scavengers have been looting them for years. Some of those engineering manuals were written by my mother.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The caches were designed to survive indefinitely, sealed against contamination, indexed against the original Concordium's administrative database — a database that the Schism split into three fragments, each faction holding a piece. The Scavengers aren't ideologically motivated; they sell to whoever pays. That means Hegemony buyers, Arcanum buyers, possibly even the Meridian Compact. Every manual they extract from those caches and sell to a rival faction is knowledge that was meant to rebuild Nexus, going instead to fragment it further. The scrap metal they collect is secondary. The information is what matters.
Five Scavengers eliminated from the reclaimed sector, two rusted scrap units recovered for materials analysis. The Concordium remembers what was buried here. We're taking it back.

I know it sounds absurd — the Concordium survived the Severance, the Schism, three rival factions, and now a rat threatens the advance. A Civil Rat Alpha has claimed the old archive building and bred a colony large enough to collapse the reclaimed sector's food supply if the infestation spreads. The irony is not lost on me. But the archive building contains something more important than the rat's pride.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: That building was a secondary administrative repository for the original unified Concordium — not the primary archive, but a significant one. Communications logs from the weeks before the Schism, engineering specifications for Cradle infrastructure that none of the three post-Schism factions have successfully reconstructed, personnel records from the founding generation. The Alpha has nested in the lower storage vaults. Its colony has been using the document storage containers as nesting material. Every week we wait, more of what's inside becomes irretrievably compromised. The Concordium does not accept that outcome.

Hunt the Alpha in the archive building, clear three Civil Scavengers from the surrounding area. Document the nesting site for our damage assessment team. Everything is data, even the cost of our failures.

Deep in the ruins, there is a Rusted Warden still patrolling. Automated guardian technology from before the Schism — programmed with the original unified Concordium's authentication protocols. It doesn't recognize our current insignia. It doesn't recognize any of the Schism's successor factions. It doesn't recognize anything except the threat-assessment protocols written into it over a century ago. And we are, by its reckoning, unauthorized personnel.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: Our engineering team tried to reprogram it. Twice. The first team was driven back before they could interface with the command core — the Warden's automated response treated the reprogramming attempt as a hostile intrusion. The second team managed to reach the interface before a power surge fried their equipment. The Warden's command protocols are hardwired to the original unified Concordium's authentication system — a system that no longer exists. The people who wrote those protocols are dead. The faction that issued those credentials fragmented into three. The machine doesn't know any of this. It just knows its orders haven't changed.

Two Rusted Wardens in the deep ruins, four Feral Scavengers cleared from the perimeter. Collect the circuit components from the Wardens for our engineering archives. The Concordium built these machines. Understanding how they failed is part of understanding what we lost.

There is a Slime King in the sewers beneath the Civil Quarter. The sewers that the Concordium's founding engineers designed as emergency water routing for a city of four million people — the infrastructure that was supposed to guarantee Nexus's survival through any crisis. It has been the throne room of a Slime King for approximately eighty years. Since shortly after the Schism, if the biological dating on the core samples is accurate.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The Slime King is not the product of the Severance's initial corruption. It grew here, in the dark, after the Schism fractured the faction that built this place. The founding engineers' greatest infrastructure achievement became an abandoned space within a generation of its construction. The Severance's contamination seeped into the neglected water system, and something coalesced in the absence of maintenance, of oversight, of care. This is what neglect looks like, given eighty years and a corrupted water supply. The Concordium's failure to hold this district together created the conditions for this creature's existence.

The Slime King in the underground cistern, two mutant hound escorts cleared. Collect the slime core — our research division wants the biological profile. End its reign, Operative. The Concordium reclaims what it built.

You've earned the right to see it. The Aethel Cradle's Outer Ring — the great machine the Concordium built around Xylos's most powerful energy source. The original engineers wept when they first activated it. I wasn't there, but I've read the field journals, and I believe that account completely. Now the Outer Ring is contested ground, and every faction wants what it contains. I need you to scout it and make sure the Concordium holds the advantage.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The Outer Ring was the Concordium's greatest engineering achievement after the Cradle reactivation itself — a network of energy capture platforms encircling the Siphon's primary output zone. Before the Great Schism, it supplied Nexus Core with enough aethel-derived power to run every system in the city indefinitely. The Schism disrupted the maintenance rotation, and now three factions fight over control of platforms that none of us has the full engineering staff to run properly. The void fragments you'll find in the flux rift sites are debris from reality-thin zones where the Cradle's energy output causes spatial instability. We use them as calibration data. They tell us how close to the edge the Outer Ring is operating.

Travel to the Outer Ring, collect three void fragments from the flux rift sites. Every data point we gather is another argument for why the Concordium — not the Hegemony, not the Arcanum — should control the Cradle. For Nexus.

The Cradle Anomaly Wolves have been sighted near the flux rifts in the Outer Ring's western sector. These aren't standard predators — they're what happens when a species spends generations in proximity to a malfunctioning aethel source. Their howls are operating on the same frequency band as our harmonic resonators. Every time a pack vocalizes near the resonators, the calibration drifts. Unchecked drift leads to cascade failure. The Concordium nearly lost the Cradle once. I refuse to let it happen again.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The resonators are what maintain stable energy output from the Outer Ring — they're the mechanism that prevents the Cradle's power from spiking into an uncontrolled surge the way it did during the Great Severance. The Aethelgardians didn't have resonator technology when they activated the Siphon. That's why the Shard shattered. We rebuilt the Siphon and added the resonator network specifically because we understood what went wrong. If the Anomaly Wolves' vocalizations interfere with that network and cause a calibration failure, we recreate the conditions of the Severance. The entire Outer Ring's operation is at risk from creatures that are themselves a product of the Severance's original damage.
Five Anomaly Wolves from the western flux rift zones, three wild hide samples for biological documentation. The Concordium's promise is that this never happens again. Help us keep it.

The Cradle Anomaly Beastlings have nested in the maintenance shafts of the Outer Ring's stabilizer array. Smaller than the Wolves, faster, and they've compromised the physical access routes our engineering teams use to maintain the stabilizers. Two maintenance rotations have been turned back already. If the array goes offline, the Arcanum Seekers will be on our doorstep before the stabilizers finish cooling.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The stabilizer array is what prevents the Outer Ring's energy output from becoming unstable during peak load cycles. Think of it as the Cradle's immune system — it detects imbalances and corrects them automatically, without human intervention. The Beastlings are probably the same species as the Anomaly Wolves at a juvenile life stage — the biological team isn't certain, but their behavioral patterns suggest it. What they share is origin: creatures shaped by sustained aethel radiation, adapted to environments that should be lethal. They're thriving in the maintenance shafts because the background radiation there would kill a standard organism in hours. It's not killing them.
Six Beastlings from the maintenance shafts, four void fragments from the rift sites they're drawn to. Our engineering teams are waiting to resume maintenance rotations. Clear the way.

The Cradle Fragment Beasts are carrying unstable aethel cores inside their bodies — literal shards of the original Aethel Shard, still radiating energy, embedded in living hosts. The Arcanum Seekers want them captured for study. The Children of the Shard want them worshipped. The Concordium wants them contained before they detonate near the Outer Ring's flux regulators and cause a cascade failure.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The prevailing theory from our biology division: in the immediate aftermath of the Severance, when the Shard shattered, the fragment scatter pattern spread across several hundred kilometers. Small organisms in the high-contamination zones ingested fragment material before the biological hazard was understood. Three centuries of biological integration have fused the fragment material with the host organism's metabolic system. The cores are still energetically active — that's why the Beasts are dangerous, and why they're drawn to the Outer Ring. The Cradle's energy output resonates with the embedded fragments. We believe they're trying to return the shards to the source. They cannot be allowed to do that.
Five Fragment Beasts from the northern sector, three aethel fragment cores recovered before detonation threshold. Ideology is secondary to operational integrity, Operative. Contain the threat.

Sector Seven has been silent for three hours. The Resonance Bats swarming the communication arrays on the Outer Ring's southern platform have effectively jammed our entire coordination network for that sector. These creatures evolved using the Cradle's own harmonic emissions as echolocation — when they pulse near the arrays, their biological signal is indistinguishable from system noise. I check the comms every thirty seconds. Nothing comes back.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The communication arrays are how the Concordium coordinates Outer Ring defense. Without them, every patrol sector operates independently, without intelligence sharing, without support coordination. Three hours of silence means three hours where a Hegemony incursion, an Arcanum infiltration, or a void breach could happen in Sector Seven and we wouldn't know until a patrol stumbled into it. The Resonance Bats are not strategically motivated — they're drawn to the array's harmonic output because it matches their echolocation frequency. But the effect is operationally equivalent to deliberate jamming. The Resonance Golems guarding their nest are amplifying the interference. Both have to go.
Eight Resonance Bats from the array perimeter, two Resonance Golems from the nest site. Restore contact with Sector Seven. The Concordium does not go dark.

Three scouts went dark in the eastern sector three days ago. I've entered their names in the operational record. Now I need you to enter the sector and recover the data they were carrying — void fragment calibration samples and an intelligence packet on Hegemony troop movements near the Cradle's loading docks. The data doesn't expire. The scouts are gone. The mission continues.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The void fragment calibration samples they carried are irreplaceable in the short term — we pulled them from a specific flux rift that has since destabilized and is no longer safely accessible. The intelligence packet was compiled over six weeks by a surveillance team I cannot replace either. The Cradle Anomaly Wolves are responsible for the scouts' situation — we found trace evidence consistent with pack behavior in the vicinity of their last reported position. I report this precisely, without additional comment, because that is what the operational record requires of me. And because the mission requires clarity, not grief.
Collect three void fragment samples from the scout site coordinates, defeat four Anomaly Wolves in the area. Bring back what they carried. The Concordium remembers its own.

A void breach has opened in the Outer Ring's north platform. Not a flux rift — a full breach, where the boundary between normal space and whatever geometry feeds the void has thinned to near-zero. Fragment Beasts are pouring through and growing stronger with each surge. Concordium engineers believe the breach appeared the same week the Iron Hegemony began unauthorized excavation near the Cradle's base. That may be coincidence. It may not.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The Hegemony's excavation team has been drilling into the Cradle's outer geological substrate — the deep rock layer where the original Aethelgardian construction anchored the Siphon's foundation. If they've penetrated the Siphon's root structure, they may have destabilized the same containment geometry that the Aethelgardians built to prevent void ingress. This is the mechanism the Concordium believes triggered the original Severance — not the Siphon overload itself, but a void geometry failure that the overload then exploited. The Hegemony either doesn't know what they've done, or they don't care. Neither possibility is acceptable.
Six Fragment Beasts near the breach site, two aethel fragments recovered before detonation. The breach will close naturally once the Fragment density drops below threshold. Move quickly, Operative.

The Resonance Golems in the deep Outer Ring have established a concentration point — not a random nest, a deliberate defensive arrangement. Concordium historians believe these constructs are the Aethel Cradle's original defense systems, built by the same Aethelgardian architects who first activated the Siphon. They're still following protocols from before recorded history. And their harmonic pulses are rewriting our communication protocols with something older and stranger.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The Golem emissions are what our signal analysis team is describing as 'pre-language' — structured harmonic patterns that predate anything in the Concordium's linguistic database. The same patterns appear in fragments of Aethelgardian inscription recovered from the Cradle's deeper chambers, but no one has been able to fully translate them. The Golems are broadcasting these patterns continuously, and our communication infrastructure is beginning to incorporate them into its error-correction responses. In six weeks, if this continues, our comm arrays will be outputting Aethelgardian protocols instead of Concordium standard. The Concordium does not discard knowledge, but neither does it allow unknown protocols to overwrite operational infrastructure.

Three Resonance Golems in the deep chambers, six Resonance Bats cleared from the surrounding area. Document the Golem emissions before you engage — every recording is data the Concordium will eventually understand.

The Resonance Echo has materialized. This is beyond the Bats and the Golems — this is a concentrated echo of the Cradle's original harmonic signature, coalesced into something that our instruments cannot categorize. The Arcanum Seekers would call it a miracle. The Children of the Shard would call it a god-fragment. I call it a threat to Concordium communication infrastructure that is currently rewriting Sector Nine's relay protocols in real time.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The best working theory from our research division: the Outer Ring's deep electromagnetic structure stores harmonic data the way biological tissue stores memory — passively, impressionistically, over centuries. What we're seeing is that stored data achieving coherent form, a self-organizing energy entity built from the Cradle's accumulated operational history. It is, in the most literal sense, a memory of the Aethel Cradle itself — before the Schism, before the Severance, possibly before human contact with the Siphon. Whatever it's trying to do, it is operating on a timeline and according to goals we cannot parse. The Concordium cannot allow unknown entities to operate autonomously within Cradle infrastructure.

The Resonance Echo at the resonance chamber, four Fragment Beasts cleared from the surrounding area. Whatever it is — miracle, god-fragment, or accumulated data — it cannot rewrite the Cradle on its own terms. Not on my watch.

The Outer Ring is not fully secured — it never will be, not while three factions and something older than all of them press against the Concordium's borders. But we hold more of it today than we did a season ago, and that is because of what you've done here. This final sweep is also a test. The Concordium is evaluating you for Advanced assignment. The Cradle's inner platforms await, and they will demand everything you have.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The Inner Cradle Exclusion Zone, the Cradle Expanse, the contested zones where all factions have active operations — these are not the Gardens, Operative. The threats there are not Slimes and Sproutlings. They're creatures that have adapted to direct Shard energy proximity, Aethelgardian defense constructs that have been operating for centuries without human oversight, void-adjacent entities that don't have biological analogs in any known database. You will need every skill you've developed in the Gardens and the Civil Quarter and the Outer Ring. This final sweep will tell us if you're ready.
Five Beastlings, five Resonance Bats, one void fragment. Final sweep of the Outer Ring's remaining hostile concentrations. Show me you're ready for what comes next, Operative.
At ease, Operative. Deep Incursion — Push deeper into the Inner Cradle Exclusion

Welcome to the Arcanum Seekers, Field Researcher. I'm High Seeker Elara Voss. The Concordium calls us reckless and the Iron Hegemony calls us dangerous — we call ourselves the only people on Xylos who are asking the right questions instead of just fortifying the answers they already have. Your first field assignment is on the Crystal Slope. The Wild Mushrooms there absorb raw aethel directly from a Shard fragment impact site. Their tissue contains energy signatures that predate the Great Severance. We need those signatures.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The Aethelgardians who built the Aethel Siphon understood aethel energy at a fundamental level that we have been reconstructing from ruins for two centuries. The Crystal Slope sits on a direct Shard fragment impact point — one of the scatter sites where the original Shard dispersed when it shattered in the Great Severance. The Wild Mushrooms that have grown over this site are not random organisms, Subject. They are biological data storage: living tissue that has absorbed and encoded pre-Severance aethel signatures over three hundred years of contact. Those signatures are our most direct access to what the Aethelgardians knew before the Siphon overloaded and destroyed everything. The fact that obtaining them requires combat is a methodology compromise, not a philosophical one.

Four Wild Mushrooms — energy signature capture at close range, then collect two mushroom caps for physical spectral analysis. The Arcanum does not apologize for how it learns. The cost of not knowing is always worse than the cost of the method. For Knowledge.

The Crystal Sproutlings on this slope are not simply obstacles, Field Researcher. I believe they are conscious. Primitively, at the level of a single persistent idea perhaps, but the energy signature data from our previous collection suggests a form of biological coherence that standard Xylos fauna don't exhibit. The Concordium classified this research as 'ethically complex' and buried it in their restricted archive. The Arcanum opened it anyway.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: Crystal Sproutlings grow from Shard fragment deposits — they are, technically, living extensions of the same material that shattered in the Great Severance. The Concordium wants them cleared as threats. The Children of the Shard would want them protected as sacred relics. We want their consciousness-signature data, because if these organisms carry biological memory encoded by the Shard's fracture pattern, that memory could tell us things about the Aethelgardians' original intent for the Siphon that no written archive has preserved. The Concordium's ethical classification is not a philosophical objection — it's administrative anxiety. There is a difference.

Four Crystal Sproutlings for consciousness-signature capture, two crystal fragments for comparative structural analysis. Bring the data intact. The Arcanum does not apologize for how it learns. For Knowledge.

I'm Archivist Sylas. I maintain what the Arcanum calls the living archive — our collection of specimens, artifacts, and data constituting institutional knowledge no single researcher can hold alone. High Seeker Voss has sent you to me because I have a hypothesis that requires specimens I cannot collect myself. The Shardlings on this slope are not random creatures. I believe each one contains a fractional encoded memory of the original Aethel Shard — a fractal pattern of the whole, distributed across biological hosts.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The fractal hypothesis began with the Sproutling consciousness data. If Sproutlings carry primitive coherent awareness derived from Shard contact, and Shardlings are organisms that developed from direct fragment integration, then the Shardling population may collectively encode the Shard's pre-fracture information state. The fracture pattern itself — the way the Shard shattered, where each fragment landed — may not have been random. The Aethelgardians may have engineered the Shard to fracture in a recoverable pattern, a fail-safe in the event the Siphon failed. If I am right, we could theoretically reconstruct the Shard's original data state from biological specimens. The Concordium has never considered this possibility because they stopped asking questions the moment the Cradle started working again.
Five Shardlings for the living archive, three crystal fragments for comparative analysis alongside the Sproutling data. Every specimen brings the reconstruction one step closer. For the Archive.

An Aethelgardian text recovered from the Resonance Caverns — one of the Arcanum's most prized possessions, classified by the Concordium and recovered during the chaos of the Schism — describes Crystal Wisps as 'light-formed messengers, carrying the speech of those who built the Siphon.' The Concordium wants them dead. The Arcanum wants their energy signatures mapped. These are different objectives, Field Researcher, and the difference matters enormously.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The Aethelgardian text provides a partial translation framework for Wisp energy patterns: they are not random light emissions but structured data bursts — the same communication protocol the Aethelgardians used to transmit technical information across the Siphon's operational network. Every Wisp that flickers out is a word from before the Severance, lost forever. Our capture equipment can preserve the energy signature at the moment of dispersal — essentially recording the Wisp's final transmission. We have been building a corpus of these recordings for six years. When we have enough, we may be able to reconstruct complete Aethelgardian technical instructions from the accumulated data. The Concordium has never attempted this because they are not interested in what the Aethelgardians were saying. Only in what they built.

Five Crystal Wisps for energy signature capture — the equipment records automatically at close range, then collect one crystal dust sample for spectral calibration. Each capture is a word we save from oblivion. For Knowledge.

I am Archsage Myr. The Observatory Spire was built by the Arcanum to watch the stars and read the aethel currents that flow between them — and because the person who designed it believed that the Aethelgardians were communicating something in the pattern of the Shard fragment scatter, and that pattern would only be visible from sufficient altitude. I have reviewed your field reports. High Seeker Voss and Archivist Sylas both recommend you. In the Arcanum, knowledge specialization is not a promotion. It is a philosophical declaration.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The Arcanum recognizes three lines of inquiry — not classes, not tactical roles, but commitments about what questions you will spend your life pursuing. The Resonance Seeker listens: studying frequencies, emissions, and the hidden communications embedded in aethel phenomena, following the Wisp translation project's methodology. The Void Architect constructs: building frameworks for understanding void energy and its relationship to the Shard, working toward practical applications the Concordium hasn't imagined. The Crystal Sage synthesizes: combining biological specimen data with Aethelgardian technical records to reconstruct lost knowledge from fragments. The wrong answer will not be punished — the Arcanum does not punish intellectual honesty. But it will shape everything you investigate from this day forward.

Speak with me to formalize your specialization, review the available paths, then demonstrate your field calibration against three Wild Mushrooms — a methodological baseline. The Spire's deeper doors are opening. Choose what questions you want to ask.

The Fractured Glass Valley was a functioning Aethelgardian research station before the Severance shattered it — and the terrain along with it. The valley's glass formations are the remnants of research structures that were conducting experiments the Concordium still cannot categorize from the fragments we've recovered. I've been trying to get a team into this valley for years. The Concordium kept classifying it restricted. Post-Schism, in the fracture of competing jurisdictions, the Arcanum moves where others hesitate. Get in before anyone else does.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The restriction was bureaucratic cowardice. The Concordium's position was that the valley's 'unstable terrain' made access 'inadvisable.' The actual reason, which their internal correspondence reveals to anyone willing to access the Arcanum's recovered archive fragments, is that the pre-Severance research conducted here involved aethel applications that the Concordium's post-Schism engineering division cannot replicate and doesn't want the general population to know about. Data slates buried in the crystalline formations contain material science experiment records that our current understanding cannot fully categorize. That is precisely why we are here. The Concordium restricts what it cannot understand. The Arcanum investigates it.

Travel to the Fractured Glass Valley and collect two stinger core samples from the valley's crystalline fauna — they carry trace data signatures from the original research station's energy field. This is the beginning, Field Researcher. For Knowledge.

I'm Archivist Sylas. I have been studying the Glass Crawlers' locomotion pattern in this valley for three months and I believe I have found something extraordinary. Their movement technique — the way they distribute weight across the razor-glass substrate without cutting themselves — follows a stress-distribution algorithm that precisely matches a partial specification recovered from the research station's structural engineering archives. They didn't evolve this solution. They inherited it.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The implication is significant. The Aethelgardian research station was conducting biological integration experiments — attempting to encode engineering solutions into living organisms' behavioral genetics. If the Glass Crawlers' locomotion is a survival of that research, it means the station's biological experiments were successful enough that the encoded behaviors persisted through three hundred years of independent evolution. The Crawlers are, in a meaningful sense, living Aethelgardian documentation — organisms that remember engineering solutions their creators recorded in their genetics. That movement data could reshape Arcanum field equipment design and tell us what else the Aethelgardians may have successfully encoded in Xylos's native fauna.

Five Glass Crawlers for movement pattern capture during the engagement — the archive kit records locomotion stress-response data automatically. Collect three desert chitin samples for comparative genetic analysis. For the Archive.

A Giant Alpha Rat has claimed the valley's sealed archive chamber as its nest. This is the chamber our scouts confirmed still contains pre-Severance data cylinders intact after three hundred years. The chamber's aethel-key sealing mechanism kept the biological contamination of the Severance out. It did not keep out a rat that found the ventilation shaft. The Concordium had this chamber on their survey maps for decades and left it sealed. The Arcanum broke through last season. A rat will not reclaim what human curiosity has finally unlocked.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The data cylinders in that chamber were sealed by Aethelgardian archivists who understood the Siphon was failing. They preserved what they could before the Great Severance destroyed everything else. Three hundred years of perfect environmental sealing — the aethel-key technology creates an atmosphere-isolated container that resists biological and energetic contamination simultaneously. The Alpha Rat's colony is using the document storage containers as nesting material. Every week the Alpha remains in that chamber, more cylinders are compromised. The Concordium's decades of neglect made this possible. The Arcanum cannot afford the same mistake.

One Giant Alpha Rat in the archive chamber, three alpha fang samples for biological documentation — the genetics team wants to know why the Alpha developed territory preference for aethel-isolated spaces. The data cylinders are rated for biological intrusion. They will survive. Go.

I'm Researcher Vin. My field specialization is energy signature analysis — I measure what things emit and attempt to understand what those emissions mean about the emitter's underlying nature. The Glass Wisps in this valley have presented me with what I can only describe as a professional emergency: their energy signatures are so complex that my standard field equipment cannot parse them. High Seeker Voss finds this exciting. I find it a calibration problem I need to solve.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: The standard Crystal Wisp emits energy in patterns consistent with simple harmonic resonance — first-order transmissions, in the Aethelgardian terminology, basically a single sustained note. The Glass Wisps are emitting recursive harmonic structures: patterns that reference themselves, that contain within them scaled-down versions of the whole emission signature. This is what the Arcanum's theoretical models predict for aethel entities that have spent generations in high-compression radiation environments — the Fractured Glass Valley's crystalline substrate concentrates ambient aethel to levels three to four times higher than the Crystal Slope. These Wisps are not just older Crystal Wisps. They are something qualitatively different, and my equipment was designed for the simpler version.

Five Glass Wisps for recursive signature capture — use the enhanced field kit I've calibrated for this assignment, not the standard equipment. Collect two glass shard samples for cross-referencing the physical-energetic correlation. This data changes the model, Field Researcher.

The Crystal Rams in the valley's deepest ravine have horns I have been studying for fourteen months. Not the keratin structure itself — the electromagnetic pattern they emit. The Rams' horns grow by absorbing the deep aethel currents flowing through the valley's crystalline substrate, the same currents that once powered Aethelgardian technology. The resulting biological structure is, functionally, an aethel conduit grown over decades into a shape that mirrors the Siphon's internal routing architecture. They solved in biology what the Aethelgardians solved in engineering.

options.text: I need to think about it.

nodes.text: I have a reconstruction project I have not shared with the Concordium and will not share with the Concordium. The project involves building a functional small-scale replica of the Siphon's internal aethel routing system using biological components — specifically, the electromagnetic pattern of the Crystal Ram horn as a template for the routing geometry. If the Aethelgardians were encoding engineering solutions in Xylos's native fauna, the Crystal Rams may be the most sophisticated example we've found. The horn's pattern is not just similar to the Siphon's routing architecture. It is structurally identical to specifications from pre-Severance construction documents that the Concordium classified before the Schism and that I recovered from their restricted archive through means I prefer not to detail.

Two Crystal Rams in the deep ravine, two crystal horns collected with the electromagnetic pattern intact — do not damage the horn tips, that is where the routing geometry terminates. This data does not reach the Concordium. For Knowledge.
